Bare "ABV" is not a TTB-prescribed notation; _check_abv_notation FAILs it (27 CFR 5.65). Allowed forms Alc./Alc/Vol./Vol/%. No spirits fixture, but the same code path is exercised by the malt fixture malt_front_abv_notation.png.

mandatory_item: Proof = 2x ABV and set off in same field of vision
notes: proof=2xABV is validated by _check_proof_consistency (27 CFR 5.65): 50 proof on 20% ABV -> FAIL (rum_front_proof_inconsistent.png). The "set off in same field of vision" layout aspect is structural (see the same-field-of-vision row), not checked.

mandatory_item: Net contents - present + standard of fill
notes: Unit-aware volume compare (PR-B): same volume in a different unit/format -> NEEDS_REVIEW; materially different volume -> FAIL; unparseable -> fuzzy fallback. The standard-of-fill table (permitted sizes, 5.203) is still NOT enforced -> partial.

Unit-aware normalization ADDED (PR-B): "16.9 FL. OZ." vs "1 PINT 0.9 FL. OZ." are the same volume -> NEEDS_REVIEW (malt_front_netcontents.png). The largest-whole-unit / standard-of-fill format rule is still not enforced -> partial.

ABV is mandatory for malt ONLY when alcohol from added flavors/non-beverage ingredients or a state requires it. The verifier treats a MISSING malt ABV as PASS (optional) and does NOT know the flavor trigger -> a flavored malt with NO ABV would be wrongly PASSED. (Our sample shows 5%, so present.)

Bare "ABV" is not an allowed abbreviation (allowed: Alc./Alc/%/Vol/Vol.); _check_abv_notation FAILs it (27 CFR 7.65). malt_front_abv_notation.png -> FAIL (value 5% still matches, but the "ABV" notation is caught).

DOCUMENTED GAP (conformance audit 2026-06-11). The malt checklist requires that IF alcohol-by-weight is stated it appear together with ABV and be accurately converted (ABV ~= 1.25 x ABW). Not modeled: the alcohol_content schema carries only abv_percent/proof; the prompt names only ABV/proof; _check_abv / _check_abv_notation / _check_proof_consistency have no ABW logic. ABW was absent from all 16 real/clearer-baseline labels in the smoke run -> low priority. A fix would add an evidence-only abw_percent extraction field + a label-only co-presence/conversion check in _check_abv (REVIEW severity, generous config tolerance), mirroring _check_proof_consistency; deferred to avoid an extraction-contract change + false-review risk for a rare requirement.
